TimeEntry.Title: JDH-HQ-PC-09 : Found critical low disk space almost zero space left on this pc
TimeEntry.Notes: I improved disk space by performing standard cleanup procedures, resulting in up to 9 GB of additional free space.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: JDH Servers pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Log in to the Manage Engine to check the servers  JDH-HQ-AD-01, JDH-HQ-VBR-01, JDH-HQ-VONE which have not been rebooted. and installed failed patches. So connected to the servers and rebooted them and verified all the services are running fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for JDH:- Sophos Firewall
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the JDH:- Sophos Firewall. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: Update failed patches on the JDH-HQ-PC-05, JDH-HQ-PC-03 & JDH-HQ-PC-08
TimeEntry.Notes:
As part of scheduled patch management, reviewed the pending updates on the below systems and installed the required Windows preview updates and BIOS update. The systems were rebooted, and a post-reboot scan was performed to confirm successful deployment.

Installed patches:

  • 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for Windows 11 Version 24H2 (x64)
    KB5074105 – Build 26100.7705

  • 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for .NET Framework 3.5 and 4.8.1 for Windows 11 24H2 (x64)
    KB5074831

  • OptiPlex SFF Plus 7020 BIOS – Version 1.22.1

All updates were applied successfully and are cleared from the ManageEngine portal.

 

JDH-HQ-PC-05, JDH-HQ-PC-03 & JDH-HQ-PC-08

TimeEntry.Title: JDH systems pending for updates after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the  ticket. Login to the manage engine portal and checked failed or missing patches  for the below mentioned machine then download and installed

the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. JDH-HQ-AD-01, JDH-HQ-VBR-01, JDH-HQ-VONE, JDH-REMOT-PC-01

After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully and machine are in healthy state.

TimeEntry.Title: JDH- Systems pending for updates after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Patch Installation and System Reboot

Systems Affected (Checked):

  1. Donald-WS-Home (User: donaldh)

  2. JDH-HQ-PC-02 (No user information)

  3. jdh-susan-dt (User: susanp)

  4. JDH-DC (User: administrator)

Patches to be Installed:

  • Microsoft Edge for Chromium business (Patch ID: 43366)

    • Description: Microsoft Edge for Chromium business (144.0.3719.104) (x64) (CVE-2026-1504)

  • 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for Windows 11 Version 24H2 (Patch ID: 113003)

    • Description: 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for Windows 11 Version 24H2 for x64-based systems (KB5074105)

  • 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for .NET Framework (Patch ID: 113008)

    • Description: Cumulative Update Preview for .NET Framework 3.5 and 4.8.1 for Windows 11, version 24H2 for x64 (KB5074831)

  • Microsoft Visual Studio Tools for Applications 2019 (Patch ID: 111366)

    • Description: Microsoft Visual Studio Tools for Applications 2019 (16.0.35907)

Tasks:

  1. Install Patches:

    • Install the listed patches on the checked systems specified above.

    • Ensure all patches are applied successfully.

  2. Reboot Systems:

    • After successful patch installation, reboot the systems remotely to apply the updates.

TimeEntry.Title: Install My RMM & Delete the old system from Myrmm
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into the myrmm & myremote portal and found the machines missing.

 

Installed the my rmm on JDH-HQ-PC-06, JDH-SUSAN-DT.

Also deleted the system Desktop-ER662A2

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam VBR server having trouble connecting with VSPC portal.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into JDH-HQ-VONE server and found the server stopped communication with central VSPC server. Troubleshoot the issue and resetup the connection with VSPC portal. Verify all the services again on the server.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.132 on JDH-HQ-AD-01, JDH-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into the Huntress portal and identified that the Huntress Agent on the following machines was running an outdated version (v0.14.82):JDH-HQ-AD-01, JDH-HQ-VONE
Connected to each machine via ManageEngine, downloaded and installed the latest Huntress Agent version V0.14.132. Verified in the Huntress portal that all systems are now updated to the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.132 on JDH-DC, JDH-HQ-VONE, JDH-HQ-WINEDGE,WINDOWS-S9LBDCA
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the Huntress portal and identified that the Huntress Agent on the following machines was running an outdated version (v0.14.82): WINDOWS-S9LBDCA, JDH-DC, JDH-HQ-VONE, JDH-HQ-WINEDGE Connected to each machine via ManageEngine, downloaded and installed the latest Huntress Agent version V0.14.132. Verified in the Huntress portal that all systems are now updated to the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version (7.33.20505.0) updated on machine:- JDH-HQ-PC-05, JDH-HQ-PC-06, WINDOWS-S9LBDCA
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines (JDH-HQ-PC-05, JDH-HQ-PC-06, WINDOWS-S9LBDCA) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.33.20505.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: JDH check for license
TimeEntry.Notes: User Hans Wang mail achieve folder is almost reaching 50gb, Checked the user profile. Suggested E3 license for the user. waiting for the JDH approval to get license for the user to get more space.

Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.33.20505.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Update failed patches on the computer JDH-HQ-PC-05,JDH-HQ-PC-06 & JDH-HQ-PC-02

Identified pending driver updates including Dell Display and Peripheral Manager (2.2.0.19) and Intel Alder Lake-S GT1 Graphics (32.0.101.7080). Connected to the systems and installed the required updates successfully. A manual scan was performed in the ManageEngine portal to verify the patches are cleared and systems are compliant.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: One drive access
TimeEntry.Notes: Received a request from Robert to provide OneDrive public share folder access for Sam Wu. The request was forwarded to Donald Hu for approval before proceeding with the access setup. Donald replied asking for clarification on which specific folder access was being requested. Awaiting confirmation from the user to proceed with the appropriate folder access configuration.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage Engine agent upgraded to the latest version 11.5.2600.19.W on JDH servers.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.19.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine (JDH-HQ-VBR-01, JDH-HQ-VONE, JDH-HQ-AD-01, JDH-HQ-WINEDGE), Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: One drive access
TimeEntry.Notes: As requested, permission has been granted for Sam Wu to access the desired folder at sharepoint. Robert has been informed to confirm with Sam whether he is able to access it or if he encounters any issues.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Hans with JDH needs assistance with his email as he isn't receiving his emails.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Hans from JDH called in regarding the Email issue...

So  connect to his system and found that he is using outlook app for email...

so closed the Outlook App and tried again but no go....

So login to email on web and tried to sent the test mail. it got delivered but not showing in Inbox..

so checked the rules and all mail were moving to RSS folder....

emails are working fine.....hence closing the ticket....

TimeEntry.Title: JDH-HQ-LPT-01 : Found a critical low disk space this pc
TimeEntry.Notes: I identified critically low disk space on the system. After performing the standard cleanup procedure, the available space has been increased to 27 GB.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Ran the Network Detective scan for JDH for the month of February.
TimeEntry.Notes: Network Detective scan for JDH has been initiated and completed for the month of February.Report generation is currently in progress. We are awaiting completion and will update the ticket once the report is generated

TimeEntry.Notes:
Had meeting with Robert. 

explained the setup

TimeEntry.Notes: Sent the adobe license users and the all the details about the license to Robert.

TimeEntry.Title: JDH-HQ-PC-02,DESKTOP-5RDSJ33,JDH-HQ-PC-03, DESKTOP-9SRRKTH,JDH-REMOT-PC-01,jdh-susan-dt Missing Patches Deployed
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines JDH-HQ-PC-02,DESKTOP-5RDSJ33,JDH-HQ-PC-03, DESKTOP-9SRRKTH,JDH-REMOT-PC-01,jdh-susan-dt. Downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed/missing patches.
Some machines rebooted successfully, but others had open user files and could not be rebooted. The machines will be rebooted after hours.
A few patches were not deployed due to compatibility issues.

Patches are OptiPlex Micro Plus 7010 (1.33.0)

Downloaded and manually installed the applicable driver updates, including:

User Hans had issues with outloook,

User had issue connecting to myremote, and user session ends every 2 hours. Its the default. Discussion start with vendor regarding the issue.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated on JDH Servers
TimeEntry.Notes: Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine JDH-DC, JDH-HQ-AD-01, JDH-HQ-VBR-01, JDH-HQ-VONE, JDH-HQ-WINEDGE running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  . Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.9.9533. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server JDH-HQ-VBR-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine

TimeEntry.Title: Ran the Network Detective scan for JDH for the month of February.
TimeEntry.Notes: Continued with the previous work. After completion, ran the reports, generated the results, and shared the update in the Teams channel undwer JDH .

TimeEntry.Title: Sage Smtp Setup
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked with Chris regarding the sage smtp setup. requested for an email. Jasmine wants to use her email, discussed with her regarding draws from using her email. gathered all the required information to fill Chris form. Waiting for jasmine response to finish the form

TimeEntry.Title: Jasmine -Sage application
TimeEntry.Notes: Fill SMTP form for the sage application using jasmine email.

TimeEntry.Title: Adobe license
TimeEntry.Notes: Update the adobe admin portal with the info provided by Robert.

TimeEntry.Title: Jasmine -Sage application
TimeEntry.Notes:
Checked the azure for the following information,

 

  1. Client ID: Enter the client ID associated with your OAuth configuration. This is typically provided when you register an application in Azure AD or the service you are using.
  2. Client Secret: Input the client secret for the registered application, which is used along with the client ID to authenticate.
  3. Token End Point: You’ll need to provide the endpoint URL that facilitates the token retrieval for authentication. For Office 365, it's usually something like:
  4. https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token
  5. Scope: This usually refers to the permissions you're granting. For sending email via Office 365, it could be:

https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.Send_offline_access

  1. Redirect URI: This is often localhost for local applications but should be set based on your system's needs.

TimeEntry.Title: JDH Pacific, Inc. | Automatically Emailing Forms from Sage
TimeEntry.Notes: I spent time creating a new Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) application specifically for SMTP OAuth authentication required by Sage. This included registering the app, assigning the correct single-tenant settings, configuring the redirect URI, and generating a secure client secret for use within Sage. After registration, I collected the necessary identifiers—including the Client ID and Tenant ID—and configured the required OAuth endpoints. I also verified the application’s authentication settings to ensure compatibility with SMTP OAuth. In addition to the app creation, I accessed the Microsoft 365 Admin Center to review Jasmine’s mailbox configuration and confirmed that Authenticated SMTP was already enabled so the mailbox could successfully send email through OAuth-based SMTP connections. All steps were completed and documented to ensure Sage can authenticate properly using Jasmine’s email account.

TimeEntry.Title: Work on the servers & firewall as all the servers were offline.
TimeEntry.Notes:
All the servers were down due to the power cut at the site.

Logged int the server once it was online, but the vone & DC server were offline.

Logged into the esxi and started the servers.

Logged into each of the servers. restarted the services and now all the servers are running healthy.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required -JDH-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged in to vCenter and found the server JDH-HQ-VONE is in Disk consolidation state.

checked the snapshots and started consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully.

Then logged into the server and verified all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Adobe and some other installation
TimeEntry.Notes: I received a call from the user and accessed his system. The user wanted to set Adobe as the default program and was unable to work with it, as it was continuously prompting for sign-in. I removed the previous installation and installed a fresh, proper package of Adobe Reader. I then set it as the default application, and it is now working fine without any sign-in prompts.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP-KND184L : Found low and critical disk space
TimeEntry.Notes: I performed standard cleanup procedures and successfully increased the available disk space to 7 GB now (Maximum possible).
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

Worked on the approved patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the missing/failed driver updates for the systems Donald-WS-Home, JDH-HQ-PC-05, JDH-HQ-PC-06 & JDH-WHOFFICE.

Downloaded and manually installed the applicable driver updates, including:

  • AlderLake-S GT1 [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_4680] (32.0.101.7082)

  • CometLake-S GT2 (UHD Graphics 630) [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_9BC5] (31.0.101.2137)

  • Comet Lake PCH CNVi WiFi [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_06F0] (24.10.3.3)

  • Ethernet Connection (11) I219-LM [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_0D4C] (12.19.2.66)

Completed installation on all systems and verified the updated driver versions through Device Manager.

Performed a post-installation scan to confirm compliance. All previously missing/failed driver updates are now successfully installed and reflecting compliant status in the ManageEngine console.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version (11.5.2600.19.W) updated:- JDH-HQ-VBR-01, JDH-HQ-PC-01, JDH-HQ-PC-06, JDH-HQ-WINEDGE
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.19.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine ( JDH-HQ-VBR-01, JDH-HQ-PC-01, JDH-HQ-PC-06, JDH-HQ-WINEDGE) and Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches JDH-REMOT-PC-01,JDH-HQ-PC-02,DESKTOP-9SRRKT
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the approved patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the missing/failed updates for the systems JDH-REMOT-PC-01, JDH-HQ-PC-02 & DESKTOP-9SRRKT.

Downloaded and manually installed the applicable driver and BIOS updates, including:

  • AlderLake-S GT1 [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_4680] (32.0.101.7082)

  • OptiPlex Micro 7020 BIOS (1.23.1)

  • Precision 3460 BIOS (3.23.1)

Completed the installation process and verified the updated driver and BIOS versions on all systems.

Performed a post-installation scan to confirm compliance. All previously missing/failed updates are now successfully installed and reflecting compliant status in the ManageEngine console.

 systems pendingfor  reboot so will be restarted after business hours to avoid user disruption.

TimeEntry.Title: Disk Utilization is 53%, threshold value for this monitor is 50% JDH-HQ-VBR-01
TimeEntry.Notes: We received an alert in My OPS regarding high disk utilization on server JDH-HQ-VBR-01. Upon investigation, disk usage was found to be above the configured threshold. I performed Disk Cleanup, cleared Windows log files, removed temporary files and templates, and deleted temporary files from the %Temp% and Prefetch folders. A total of 17 GB of disk space was successfully freed, which significantly reduced disk utilization and improved overall system performance. The issue has been resolved, and the ticket has been closed.

TimeEntry.Title: JDH-HQ-PC-03: SNMP Installation and Configuration
TimeEntry.Notes: During diagnosis in My OPS Portal, it was found that JDH-HQ-PC-03 had not been syncing due to a missing SNMP installation and/or a mismatched probe string. I installed the SNMP service, configured the required SNMP community and thread settings, and then rediscovered and re-added the device to the monitoring system. After these steps, the machine is now properly synced with OpManager, and alerts are being displayed correctly.

TimeEntry.Title: JDH-HQ-PC-05: SNMP Installation and Configuration
TimeEntry.Notes: During diagnosis in My OPS Portal, it was found that JDH-HQ-PC-05 had not been syncing due to a missing SNMP installation and/or a mismatched probe string. I installed the SNMP service, configured the required SNMP community and thread settings, and then rediscovered and re-added the device to the monitoring system. After these steps, the machine is now properly synced with OpManager, and alerts are being displayed correctly.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated on machine WINDOWS-S9LBDCA,SUPPLYSALESCONF,JDH-SUSAN-DT,JDH-REMOT-PC-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine WINDOWS-S9LBDCA,SUPPLYSALESCONF,JDH-SUSAN-DT,JDH-REMOT-PC-01 running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine. Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.9.9533. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Replication was stopped within Active Directories.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the active directory servers and then troubleshoot and fix the issue with replication on AD servers. Once the issue has been resolved then verify all the services are up and replication is up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP-ER662A2- Device Down: No response from device for last 5 polls
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine DESKTOP-ER662A2 has no response from last 5 polls.
Checked the machines in all the portals and didn't find that . As a result, removed the machine from OP Manager portal.

TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP-S0B7F7D- Critical alert for machine in OPS portal
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine DESKTOP-S0B7F7D has critical alerts.
Checked the machines in all the portals and didn't find that . As a result, removed the machine from OP Manager portal.

TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP Alert - Attention - Frontdesk-JDH.jdh.com$MonitorField(monitorName)
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine Frontdesk-JDH has SNMP credentials failed.
So login into the portal and upon investigation, found that the IP address of machine was different which was on portal. So updated the IP address
of machine on portal and refresh the device on OP manager portal. After completing these steps, all sensors resumed normal operation. As a result,
 closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 25.9.9.9533) update:- WINDOWS-S9LBDCA, SUPPLYSALESCONF, JDH-REMOT-PC-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the devices (WINDOWS-S9LBDCA, SUPPLYSALESCONF, JDH-REMOT-PC-01) were running an outdated version of Screen Connect. I initiated the update for these devices from the portal, but after the update, I noticed some issues when verifying the version on the portal. connected to the machines through My Rmm and uninstall the screen connect software through Revo uninstaller. after that download and install the fresh MyRemote setup and install on it  and verify that agents have been updated successfully with the latest version(v 25.9.5.9483).). Hence closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.33.20505.0)updated on machines DESKTOP-8QP97UG,FRONTDESK-JDH,JDH-HQ-PC-03,JDH-HQ-PC-09
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines DESKTOP-8QP97UG,FRONTDESK-JDH,JDH-HQ-PC-03,JDH-HQ-PC-09 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.33.20505.0 of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required -JDH-HQ-WINEDGE
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server JDH-HQ-WINEDGE is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Hans with JDH needs assistance with his outlook email and his sending of emails
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the user’s call, connected to the user’s machine and resolved the Outlook issue. The user was unable to see Sent Items. After troubleshooting, the issue was fixed.

TimeEntry.Title: Critical Alarm: We are getting alarm on JDH-HQ-AD-01 for "interface 'WAN Miniport(SSTP)-Local Area Connection* 1' is shutdown"
TimeEntry.Notes:
We are getting alarm on JDH-HQ-AD-01 for "interface 'WAN Miniport(SSTP)-Local Area Connection* 1' is shutdown"

Its up now.

TimeEntry.Title: Critical Alarm: Device Down No response from device for last polls > JDH-HQ-PC-09
TimeEntry.Notes:
Critical Alarm: Device Down No response from device for last polls > JDH-HQ-PC-09

Now Polling status is Active.

TimeEntry.Title: JDH - Sophos Firewall Firmware Update - SFOS 22.0.0 GA-Build411
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Heading: JDHSophos Firewall Firmware Update - SFOS 22.0.0 GA-Build411

Ticket Summary:

Description:
The Sophos Firewall (SFOS 21.5.1 MR-1) is currently running on build 261. The latest available firmware version, SFOS 22.0.0 GA-Build411, needs to be downloaded and applied to update the firewall system.

Action Required:

  1. Verify the latest firmware version (SFOS 22.0.0 GA-Build411) is available.

  2. Download SFOS 22.0.0 GA-Build411.

  3. Apply the firmware update and monitor the system for any issues during the installation process.

  4. Ensure that a backup is taken before proceeding with the update.
